ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (PER ORDER FILED AUG. 16, 2010) HEARING SET FOR SEP-20-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED GRANTING RESPONDENTS' EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER EXTENDING TIME FOR FILING ITS OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND CONTINUING HEARING DATE FILED BY PETITIONER KOLLAR, MANFRED P. MALIM, GEORGE
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-20-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PETITIONERS MANFRED P. KOLLAR AND GEORGE MALIM'S PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE LANGUAGE OF SECTION 45310.6 IS CLEAR TO APPLY ONLY WHEN AN EMPLOYEE LEAVES A RECIPROCITY RETIREMENT SYSTEM IS IN EFFECT AT THE TIME OF THE DISCONTINUANCE OF THE EMPLOYMENT. PETITIONERS LEFT THE RETIREMENT SYSTEM BEFORE THE RECIPROCITY PROVISIONS WERE IN EFFECT AND THUS DID NOT LEAVE UNDER THE SYSTEM OF RECIPROCITY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN C. MCCASLIN OF GOYETTE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916) 851-1900 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. REBECCA LIU, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3870 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT CCSF.

RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PETITIONERS MANFRED P. KOLLAR AND GEORGE MALIM'S Petition For Declaratory Relief And For Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. THE LANGUAGE OF SECTION 45310.6 IS CLEAR TO APPLY ONLY WHEN AN EMPLOYEE LEAVES A RECIPROCITY RETIREMENT SYSTEM IS IN EFFECT AT THE TIME OF THE DISCONTINUANCE OF THE EMPLOYMENT. PETITIONERS LEFT THE RETIREMENT SYSTEM BEFORE THE RECIPROCITY PROVISIONS WERE IN EFFECT AND THUS DID NOT LEAVE UNDER THE SYSTEM OF RECIPROCITY. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =(301/PJB)
